title: Effect of Electronic Nursing Documentation Systems on Documentation Completeness and Patient Safety Outcomes: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"sonfn","text":"Electronic nursing documentation systems are widely implemented to improve the quality, completeness, and accessibility of patient information. However, evidence regarding their measurable impact remains inconsistent. Previous reviews are largely narrative and lack quantitative synthesis. Many studies focus on general EHR use rather than nursing-specific documentation, and outcomes such as care quality are inconsistently defined. This review aims to provide a systematic synthesis with limited meta-analysis focusing on measurable outcomes such as documentation completeness and patient safety indicators.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Formal screening of search results against eligibility criteria.
Records managed via reference software; screening and extraction conducted independently by two reviewers using standardized forms.
ROBINS-I for non-randomized studies and Cochrane RoB 2 for randomized studies.
Random-effects meta-analysis using OR and SMD where applicable; otherwise narrative synthesis.
By outcome type and study design where sufficient data are available.
Excluding high risk of bias and borderline studies where applicable.
Results will be published in a peer-reviewed journal and presented at conferences.
creator: aljomaie.hassan
